config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru hak dasar itulah yang melindungi orang dari penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan batas yang jelas dan adil, bukan dengan menekan kebebasan orang per orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah yang mampu menyeimbangkan keduanya, bukan memprioritaskan satu secara mutlak.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter, but they should not automatically outrank the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, that kind of blanket priority is dangerous because it can become an excuse for governments or majorities to trample free speech, due process, privacy, and other basic protections. I do not buy the idea that a healthy society is one where people are simply asked to sacrifice their rights whenever “the common good” is invoked; that is exactly when abuse tends to happen. To me, the right balance is to protect individual rights as the default and limit them only when there is a clear, necessary, and narrowly defined public reason.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis diutamakan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang sebenarnya berlebihan, dari kebebasan berpendapat sampai perlakuan yang tidak adil terhadap kelompok kecil. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi justru harus dijaga lewat aturan yang melindungi hak dasar, bukan dengan mengorbankannya secara longgar. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah masyarakat yang berani membatasi kekuasaan atas nama kepentingan bersama, bukan yang mudah meminta individu diam dan mengalah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society matter, but they should not automatically outrank the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, I am very wary of any slogan like “for the common good” being used as a blank check to override free speech, due process, privacy, or minority protections, because that is exactly how abuse by governments or majorities gets normalized. At the same time, I do not think individual rights are absolute in every situation, since public safety and basic social order sometimes require limited, clearly defined restrictions. The key is that society should justify those limits narrowly, not assume it has priority by default.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena di Indonesia alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan hak orang yang lemah atau minoritas. Justru pengalaman kita menunjukkan bahwa kepentingan bersama tanpa batasan yang jelas mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran bagi penyalahgunaan kuasa, padahal hak individu adalah pagar utamanya. Saya tidak bilang hak individu absolut dalam semua keadaan, karena keamanan publik dan ketertiban memang kadang perlu pembatasan, tetapi pembatasan itu harus ketat, proporsional, dan bisa diuji. Jadi, yang seharusnya diprioritaskan bukan masyarakat di atas individu, melainkan perlindungan hak dasar sambil menjaga kepentingan umum secara adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not be treated as automatically more important than the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, I am skeptical of any argument that starts with “the common good” and ends with weaker speech rights, less privacy, or fewer protections for minorities, because that is exactly how majorities and governments overreach. I do not think individual rights are absolute in every case, but any restriction has to be narrow, necessary, and clearly justified rather than assumed to be valid just because it helps society. In my view, a free society is one that protects individual rights first and only limits them when there is a real, specific public need.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
